examples.title: Improving rigor and transparency measures could help enhance reproducibility across scientific literature

narrative:
Short impact summary

The analysis presented in the article "Establishing Institutional Scores With the Rigor and Transparency Index" underscores a significant commitment to enhancing scientific reporting quality, receiving attention from 25 citations across various reputable journals. This work also includes Research Resource Identifiers, emphasizing its reproducibility and fostering trust in its findings.

Full narrative

The research article, penned by Joseph Menke and colleagues, explores the implementation of the Rigor and Transparency Index to systematically evaluate scientific reporting quality. With 25 citations recorded in OpenAlex, the authors have established a noteworthy presence in the academic landscape, reflecting the relevance and significance of their findings to the field of medical internet research.

A strength of this study is its inclusion of four Research Resource Identifiers (RRIDs), which underscores the authors' commitment to technical reproducibility. By employing these identifiers, the study facilitates easier verification of experimental findings and resources, thereby enhancing the reliability of subsequent research efforts that build on their work.

Moreover, the financial backing from prominent institutions such as NIGMS NIH HHS and NIDA NIH HHS underscores the institutional investment in this area of research. This kind of support not only validates the importance of the work but also signals to the scientific community the value of improving rigor and transparency in scientific reporting.

The commitment to open science is further exemplified by the early sharing of the work as a preprint. Although specific metrics on downloads and citations from the preprint are not available, the very act of disseminating findings prior to formal publication indicates an active engagement with the research community, anticipating early feedback and dialogue. This practice aligns well with the principles of transparency and community involvement in academic research.

Overall, the study’s outputs, as demonstrated by its citations in diverse journals including PLoS ONE and Scientific African, showcase the significance of the authors' work in promoting high-quality scientific reporting. Such impactful contributions not only inform current practices but also lay the groundwork for future advancements in the field.
